rationale: In the citing paper, Brown et al. (2002) appears as a single background citation in the hypothesis development for H4, used as an example for why customer orientation might increase job satisfaction. The paper does not discuss that study's model, measures, or findings in detail, and it is not used as a methodological or empirical basis for the meta-analysis itself. Because the citation serves only a brief illustrative framing role without a distinct ongoing function, it is best classified as a passing mention.
